card.intuition: A one-dimensional layout model that distributes space among items in a container, allowing them to shrink or grow to fill available space along a main axis.
card.analogy: A line of people at a bus stop: the bus stop (Flex Container) can tell people to stand in a row (row) or a column (column). If there is extra room, people can spread out (justify-content), and if it's too crowded, they can squeeze together (flex-shrink).
when_to_use: Aligning items in a navigation bar
Creating simple, responsive one-dimensional rows or columns
gotchas: The 'Main Axis' changes depending on `flex-direction`. If direction is `column`, `justify-content` controls vertical alignment, not horizontal
Flex items by default have `min-width: auto`, which can sometimes prevent them from shrinking as much as you expect

exercise.prompt: Identify the CSS property used to align flex items along the CROSS axis (perpendicular to the main axis).
hints: It starts with 'a' and rhymes with 'assign'
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "align"
assert check("align")
print("PASS")
